REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT STEPHEN WILLIAMS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF SFRO 37.10B. THIS PROVISION OF THE ORDINANCE PROHIBITS CERTAIN ACTS TO BE COMMITTED BY CERTAIN PEOPLE ("NO LANDLORD, AND NO AGENT, CONTRACTOR, SUBCONTRACTOR OR EMPLOYEE OF THE LANDLORD") AND ALLOWS FOR A CIVIL ACTION TO BE BROUGHT FOR ITS VIOLATION. THE DUTIES AND LIABILITIES OF EACH OF THE PERSONS LISTED ARE SEPARATE AND APART FROM THE LIABILITIES OF OTHERS, I.E. LANDLORD IS PROHIBITED FROM COMMITTING THE ACTS AND AGENT (E.G. PROPERTY MANAGER) IS PROHIBITED FROM COMMITTING THE SAME ACTS. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-12-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel Berko, Esq. (415-771-6174) for Plaintiff/opposing party Laura Hermosillo.
Aaron T. Schutz, Esq. (925-930-9090) of Galloway Lucchese for Defendant/Moving party STEPHEN WILLIAMS.

The Court announced Tentative Ruling. Plaintiff submits to tentative ruling. Both sides ARGUED re: the term "agent," CC 52.1 and using State Law versus Federal Law. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STEPHEN WILLIAMS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERULED in part.

Demurrer is OVERRULED as to the cause of action for violation of SFRO 37.10B. This provision of the ordinance prohibits certain acts to be committed by certain people ("No landlord, and no agent, contractor, subcontractor or employee of the landlord") and allows for a civil action to be brought for its violation. The duties and liabilities of each of the persons listed are separate and apart from the liabilities of others, i.e. landlord is prohibited from committing the acts and agent (e.g. property manager) is prohibited from committing the same acts. This independent liability is sufficiently alleged (as distinguished from aiding and abetting or conspiracy, which cannot be asserted without compliance with CC CC 1714.10; Howard v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 2 Cal.App.4th 745).
Demurrer for cause of action under CC 52.1 is OVERRULED. Violation of Bane Act is sufficiently alleged.

Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for trespass to chattels for Plaintiff to allege actual damages suffered by reason of the impairment of the property of the loss of its use.

Prevailing party (Plaintiff) to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)
